title: Global AI Governance Framework Comparison
description: Side-by-side comparison of all 11 frameworks across key governance dimensions.

eu-ai-act.detail: Directly applicable EU law. Phased enforcement 2024-2027. Penalties up to 35M EUR / 7% global turnover.

china-genai.detail: Administrative regulation. Penalties under existing cyber/data security laws.

malaysia-ngaige.detail: Non-binding ethical guidelines issued by MOSTI. No enforcement mechanism.

nist-ai-rmf.detail: Non-regulatory guidance. Referenced by some US procurement and policy requirements.

iso-42001.detail: Voluntary but certifiable. Increasingly required by enterprise procurement and government contracts.

singapore-maigf.detail: Non-binding. IMDA may incentivize adoption through AI Verify testing.

oecd-ai-principles.detail: Non-binding recommendation adopted by 46 countries. Influences national legislation.

unesco-ai-ethics.detail: Non-binding. Adopted by 193 member states. Moral authority but no enforcement.

uk-ai-framework.detail: Initially non-statutory. Existing regulators apply principles within their domains.

us-ai-policy.detail: Binding on federal agencies only. Does not directly regulate private sector.

canada-ai.detail: AIDA legislation died in Parliament. Only voluntary code remains.

eu-ai-act.detail: Prohibited, high-risk (Annex III), limited risk, minimal risk, plus GPAI with systemic risk tier.

china-genai.detail: Scope limited to generative AI services. No tiered classification.

nist-ai-rmf.detail: Risk assessment throughout lifecycle via MAP and MEASURE functions. No fixed categories.

iso-42001.detail: Requires risk assessment (6.1) but criteria defined by implementing organization.

uk-ai-framework.detail: Risk assessment by individual regulators within their domains.

us-ai-policy.detail: Deregulatory approach. Removed EO 14110 dual-use model reporting.

canada-ai.detail: AIDA would have defined 'high-impact AI systems' by regulation.

eu-ai-act.value: All AI systems in EU market
eu-ai-act.detail: Provider/deployer obligations. Extraterritorial effect for systems placed on EU market.

china-genai.detail: Text, image, audio, video generation services provided to the public in mainland China.

malaysia-ngaige.detail: Developers, providers, users across all sectors. Voluntary adoption.

nist-ai-rmf.detail: Technology-neutral. Applicable to any AI system by any organization voluntarily.

iso-42001.detail: AIMS certification scope defined by organization. Any sector.

oecd-ai-principles.detail: Governments and AI actors across the lifecycle.

unesco-ai-ethics.detail: Applies to member states' AI policies and governance.

uk-ai-framework.detail: Existing regulators apply to AI in their sectors.

us-ai-policy.detail: Binding on federal agencies. Private sector through procurement.

canada-ai.detail: Only organizations that voluntarily adopt the code.

eu-ai-act.detail: Art. 13 instructions for use, Art. 50 deepfake/AI disclosure, Art. 85 right to explanation.

china-genai.detail: Art. 12 requires watermarks/metadata on synthetic content.

iso-42001.detail: A.4.3 notification, A.4.4 disclosure, A.4.5 explainability.

us-ai-policy.detail: Deregulatory order does not include transparency mandates.

eu-ai-act.detail: Up to 35M EUR / 7% turnover. AI Office for GPAI, national authorities for others.

china-genai.detail: Warnings, fines, service suspension, criminal liability under existing laws.

malaysia-ngaige.detail: Voluntary guidelines with no enforcement mechanism.

nist-ai-rmf.detail: No enforcement. Market incentive through adoption.

iso-42001.detail: Third-party audit. Market-driven adoption via procurement requirements.

singapore-maigf.detail: AI Verify testing framework. No penalties.

uk-ai-framework.detail: Sectoral regulators enforce within their existing powers.

us-ai-policy.detail: Binding on federal agencies via OMB directives.

canada-ai.detail: Code is non-binding. No enforcement mechanism.
